$comment: Davidup hello-world — the design-doc §3.1 logo fade-in.
What it does: 3-second clip on a 1280×720 / 60fps canvas. A rounded-rectangle 'logo' fades in
(opacity 0→1, easeOutQuad, 0–1.0s) and pops in scale (0.5→1.0, easeOutBack, 0–1.5s on both axes).
After 1.5s every tween is at its 'to' value and the resolver holds it there until t=duration.
Why a shape and not an image: this composition is intentionally zero-asset so it runs out of the
box (validation passes, render-to-mp4 works without any external file). To use a real logo
instead, register an image asset and swap the 'logo' item to type 'sprite'. See examples/render.ts
and examples/mcp-demo.md for the asset-bearing variant.
Coordinate system: origin top-left, y-down (per §1). Anchor (0.5, 0.5) means the logo's transform
x/y is its CENTER, and rotation/scale pivot around the center.
$comment is non-canonical metadata: the schema's catch-all (z.record on items, z.object passthrough
on the root) ignores unknown keys, so this comment block is preserved on parse but never rendered.
It exists purely so this file documents itself when read in isolation. Strip it if you copy the
JSON into an environment that complains about extra fields.
